rulings.Case Title: DAN CLAYTON THOMPSON VS. ASESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JULY 03, 2008 LINE 2. DEFENDANT SQUARE D COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: DAN CLAYTON THOMPSON VS. ASESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JULY 03, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT CISSELL MANUFACTURING COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION. DEFENDANT FAILS TO NEGATE PLAINTIFF'S TESTIMONY AT PAGES 60 - 63 AND 466. THE SALAMEH DECLARATION LACKS FOUNDATION AND IS VAGUE AND CONCLUSIONARY. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATION ARE SUSTAINED. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: DAN CLAYTON THOMPSON VS. ASESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JULY 03, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT 84 LUMBER COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. FAILED TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT'S RELIANCE ON PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY IS INSUFFICIENT TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. VILLA V MCFINEN (1995) 35 CAL APP 4TH 733. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: DAN CLAYTON THOMPSON VS. ASESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JULY 03, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION IS ADMISSIBLE AND CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT ON LIABILITY ISSUES. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE KING et al VS. AC AND S,INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JULY 03, 2008 LINE 3. DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS UNABLE TO PRESENT EVIDENCE LINKING DECEDENT TO AN ASBESTOS PART MANUFACTURED OR SUPPLIED BY DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE OF DECEDENT BEING EXPOSED TO DUST FROM DEFENDANT'S VEHICLES BUT THAT IS NOT SUFFICIENT TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE DUST CONTAINED ASBESTOS FROM A PART, MANUFACTURED OR SUPPLIED BY DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS ARE SUSTAINED EXCEPT AS TO OBJECTION FOUR. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES SCHWAB & CO VS. LARRY YU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JULY 03, 2008 LINE 6. DEFENDANTS LARRY YU, CARLY FONG, PHILLIP SHINN, THORTON, TAYLOR, BECKER & SHINN'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES IS GRANTED IN PART AND CONTINUED IN PART FOR FURTHER BRIEFING. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO MR. HENRY'S MOTION FOR FEES AND COSTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $17,586.67. THE MOTION IS CONTINUED TO JULY 21, 2008 FOR THE MOVING PARTIES TO ADDRESS THE FOLLOWING ISSUES: (1) MR. HENRY'S WORK IN RESPONDING TO SUBPOENA'S ON OCTOBER 29 AND 31 AND NOVEMBER 6, OPPOSITION TO O.S.C. ON NOVEMBER 20 AND C.M.C. ON MARCH 17. (2) BECKER AND POTTER TO ADDRESS WHAT WORK IS PROPERLY ALLOCATED TO MR. SHINN AS CONTRASTED WITH THE FIRM PER WITTE V KAUFMAN (2006) 141 CAL APP 4TH 1201. SHINN'S FEE REQUEST IS DENIED BASED ON WITTE V KAUFMAN. =(302/PJM/VC)
